title: Stroke Pathway Activation Windowing
description: An acute-care operations workflow that coordinates urgent intake, prioritization, and constrained resource assignment under clinical safety rules. Focus case: stroke pathway activation windowing.
assumptions: Resources modeled as discrete, mutually exclusive reservations (a unit cannot be double-booked).
Teletriage and notification actions represent atomic communications that establish or release virtual connectivity.
Procedure slots and team assignments are required preconditions for definitive intervention scheduling.
Patient categories (pre_hospital_patient vs teletriage_patient) capture different entry routes and allowable workflows.
risks: Resource contention: simultaneous activations may exhaust critical resources (imaging, teams, beds) leading to delayed treatment.
Time-window expiry: delays in registration, transport, or resource reservation can render patients ineligible for time‑sensitive therapies.
Incomplete gating: failing to perform required preprocedure checks risks unsafe or suboptimal interventions.
Incorrect release/reassignment: improper freeing of resources may create false availability and subsequent allocation conflicts.
